name: UM Game Playing Strength of MCTS - 1st Place (2024) - Pipeline Template
summary: Tabular regression solution template for UM Game Playing Strength of MCTS - 1st Place (2024), modeled as a 6-stage deterministic pipeline: Feature Augmentation, Target Smoothing, Quantile Regression Training (Lower), Quantile Regression Training (Upper), and downstream competition-specific post-processing.
dejargonized_summary: This template captures the ordered steps used by UM Game Playing Strength of MCTS - 1st Place (2024): prepare the data, build task-specific representations, train or apply the main model, and convert outputs into the final competition submission.

inputs.name: raw_competition_data
inputs.constraints: Raw data for a Tabular regression task.

outputs.name: solution_predictions
outputs.constraints: Final predictions formatted for the benchmark metric.

use_when: The new task has the same broad problem type: Tabular regression.
The available data supports the same ordered transformations and intermediate artifacts as the documented winning pipeline.
Offline batch processing is acceptable, including feature extraction, model ensembling, or post-processing stages.
do_not_use_when: The target task lacks the modality or metadata required by the early pipeline stages.
Strict real-time inference is required and the template depends on heavyweight extraction, retrieval, ensembling, or test-time augmentation.
The evaluation metric rewards a fundamentally different output structure than the source competition.
applicability.key_insight: The load-bearing idea is the pipeline topology: Feature Augmentation creates the representation needed by 8/0.2 weighting schema (numpy)., so later stages can optimize the competition metric without solving raw-data ingestion from scratch.

swappable_stages.8_0_2_weighting_schema_numpy: The final model or ensemble can often be replaced if it preserves the same input/output contract and metric semantics.

applicability.scaling_notes: Scale bottlenecks are usually in feature extraction, nearest-neighbor/retrieval stages, neural model inference, and ensemble evaluation; cache intermediate artifacts when adapting this CDG.
failure_modes: Distribution shift breaks handcrafted thresholds, post-processing rules, or validation-derived weights.
Missing metadata prevents faithful construction of candidates, groups, windows, or cross-validation folds.
A weak first-stage recall step cannot be recovered by downstream rescoring or ensembling.

planning_constraints.subject: preserve_stage_order
planning_constraints.statement: Pipeline stages must execute in the documented order unless a replacement stage preserves the same input/output artifact contract.
planning_constraints.rationale: Downstream stages assume artifacts produced by earlier transformations.

planning_constraints.subject: validation_leakage_guard
planning_constraints.statement: Splits, folds, time ordering, and group boundaries from the source task must be respected when adapting the template.
planning_constraints.rationale: Many winning pipelines depend on leakage-safe validation or temporal grouping.

stages.name: Feature Augmentation
stages.description: Recompute all non-deterministic search tree features 10 distinct times and sample directly from non-held-out runs. This topological stage intentionally injects structural noise into the training rows to combat overfitting (pandas, numpy)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage recompute all non-deterministic search tree features 10 distinct times and sample directly from non-held-out runs. This topological stage intentionally injects structural noise into the training rows to combat overfitting (pandas, numpy).

inputs.name: raw_competition_data
inputs.constraints: Raw inputs for UM Game Playing Strength of MCTS - 1st Place (2024).

outputs.name: feature_augmentation_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Target Smoothing
stages.description: Recompute the competition's target labels 5 times to estimate a significantly cleaner, lower-variance "real" target value
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage recompute the competition's target labels 5 times to estimate a significantly cleaner, lower-variance "real" target value.

inputs.name: target_smoothing_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: target_smoothing_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Quantile Regression Training (Lower)
stages.description: Train a regressor explicitly targeted toward the negative (-1) direction utilizing a strict quantile loss function (alpha=0.2) (lightgbm or xgboost)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage train a regressor explicitly targeted toward the negative (-1) direction utilizing a strict quantile loss function (alpha=0.2) (lightgbm or xgboost).

inputs.name: quantile_regression_training_lower_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: quantile_regression_training_lower_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Quantile Regression Training (Upper)
stages.description: Train an independent regressor targeted toward the positive (+1) direction using an inverse quantile loss (alpha=0.8)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage train an independent regressor targeted toward the positive (+1) direction using an inverse quantile loss (alpha=0.8).

inputs.name: quantile_regression_training_upper_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: quantile_regression_training_upper_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Directional Ensembling
stages.description: Blend the initial negative predictions heavily with the alpha=0.2 model and positive predictions with the alpha=0.8 model using a fixed
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage blend the initial negative predictions heavily with the alpha=0.2 model and positive predictions with the alpha=0.8 model using a fixed.

inputs.name: directional_ensembling_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: directional_ensembling_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: 8/0.2 weighting schema (numpy).

inputs.name: 8_0_2_weighting_schema_numpy_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: solution_predictions
outputs.constraints: Final predictions or structured outputs for the competition metric.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

edges.provenance: Feature Augmentation output is consumed by Target Smoothing.
Target Smoothing output is consumed by Quantile Regression Training (Lower).
Quantile Regression Training (Lower) output is consumed by Quantile Regression Training (Upper).
Quantile Regression Training (Upper) output is consumed by Directional Ensembling.
Directional Ensembling output is consumed by 8/0.2 weighting schema (numpy)..
